title: Input and Type Conversion
summary: Use input, int, float, and str to handle user-entered values.

sketchBlocks.title: Getting User Input
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In Python, you can use the `input()` function to get input from the user. For example:

```python
name = input('What is your name? ')
print('Hello, ' + name + '!')
```

This code asks the user for their name and then greets them. 

**Your turn:** Try modifying the prompt to ask for your favorite color.

sketchBlocks.title: Type Conversion
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When you get input from the user, it is always in string format. You can convert it to other types using functions like `int()` and `float()`. For example:

```python
age = int(input('How old are you? '))
print('In 5 years, you will be', age + 5)
```

This code converts the user's age from a string to an integer so you can do math with it.

**Try it yourself:** Change the code to ask for your height in meters and print it in centimeters.

quizDraft.title: Convert User Input to Integer
quizDraft.prompt: Write a program that asks the user for their age and prints out the age in 10 years.
quizDraft.starterCode:
age = input('Enter your age: ')
# Convert age to an integer
quizDraft.solutionCode:
age = int(input('Enter your age: '))
print('In 10 years, you will be', age + 10)

quizDraft.prompt: The function used to convert a string to an integer is `______`.

quizDraft.title: Understanding Input Types
quizDraft.prompt: What type is the value returned by the input function?

quizDraft.title: Type Conversion Functions
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following functions can be used for type conversion in Python? (Select all that apply)

quizDraft.title: Convert User Input to Float
quizDraft.prompt: Write a program that asks the user for a number and prints it as a float.
quizDraft.starterCode:
number = input('Enter a number: ')
# Convert number to a float
quizDraft.solutionCode:
number = float(input('Enter a number: '))
print('The number is:', number)
